description_long: This bungalow features a steeply pitched, large hip roof with a muted brown color, providing substantial coverage over the structure. The exterior walls are a light beige with subtle brick accents, set atop a gray stone foundation that adds visual weight and stability. A small, covered porch with a gabled roof extends from one side, supported by simple posts and accessed by a short set of steps. The building appears to be a single-story dwelling, typical of suburban or residential neighborhoods. There are no visible windows or doors in the provided views, giving the structure a minimalist and somewhat utilitarian appearance. The overall design is functional, with clean lines and a focus on shelter and simplicity.
description: A single-story bungalow with a large hip roof, beige walls, and a small covered porch, set on a gray stone foundation. The design is simple and functional, with minimal exterior detailing.
description_view_0: Side view showing the main hip roof and a partial view of the covered porch extending from the right.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the entrance porch with its gabled roof and supporting posts.
description_view_2: Opposite side view focusing on the hip roof and the porch structure extending to the left.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the uninterrupted hip roof and plain beige wall with no visible openings.
